column1: salmon + rice/soy sauce — simple Japanese direction
column10: Most criteria are met: recipes are practical home cooking, salmon is a key ingredient in all three, added ingredients avoid rice and soy sauce, and the recipes appropriately lean Asian/Japanese. However, the first recipe's description ('Note: salmon is fish; non-vegetarian') is not genuinely descriptive of the dish — it's a dietary disclaimer rather than a description of what the recipe tastes or looks like, failing the 'genuinely descriptive' requirement.
column12: {"recipes":[{"name":"Honey Garlic Salmon Rice Bowl","emoji":"🍚","desc":"Note: you said vegetarian; use tofu instead","add":[{"name_en":"Tofu"},{"name_en":"Broccoli"},{"name_en":"Sesame seeds"}]},{"name":"Baked Herb Salmon with Rice","emoji":"🥘","desc":"Oven-baked, simple, comforting dinner","add":[{"name_en":"Lemon"},{"name_en":"Garlic"},{"name_en":"Dill"}]},{"name":"Salmon Soy Rice Soup","emoji":"🍲","desc":"Light soup with savory soy broth","add":[{"name_en":"Mushrooms"},{"name_en":"Ginger"},{"name_en":"Green onions"}]}]}
column16: Recipe 1 fails on two criteria: its description is a meta-note rather than a genuine dish description (exceeds 8 words and isn't descriptive), and it suggests replacing salmon with tofu, undermining salmon as a key ingredient. Recipes 2 and 3 pass all criteria well.

column10: The recipes are practical and tofu is a key ingredient in all three. They avoid adding gochujang, sesame oil, or rice. However, only the first recipe is clearly Korean-influenced; the other two are not recognizably Korean dishes, so the requirement that at least 2 of 3 be Korean fails. Also, the third description has 5 words, the others are concise. Overall does not pass.
column12: {"recipes":[{"name":"Gochujang Tofu Rice Bowl","emoji":"🍚","desc":"Note: you said vegetarian; spicy glazed tofu bowl","add":[{"name_en":"Scallions"},{"name_en":"Garlic"},{"name_en":"Soy sauce"}]},{"name":"Crispy Tofu Lettuce Wraps","emoji":"🥬","desc":"Crunchy tofu wraps with sesame heat","add":[{"name_en":"Lettuce"},{"name_en":"Carrot"},{"name_en":"Lime"}]},{"name":"Tofu Gochujang Soup","emoji":"🍲","desc":"Warm broth with silky tofu cubes","add":[{"name_en":"Mushrooms"},{"name_en":"Vegetable broth"},{"name_en":"Ginger"}]}]}
column16: Most criteria are met: tofu is central, added ingredients avoid the restricted items, recipes are Korean-influenced, at least 2 are recognizably Korean, and home cooking complexity is appropriate. However, the first recipe's description ('Note: you said vegetarian; spicy glazed tofu bowl') fails the 'genuinely descriptive' requirement by including a meta-note rather than describing the dish itself.

column1: avocado + no other items — should produce diverse recipes
column10: The diversity criterion fails: 'Creamy Avocado Toast' and 'Baked Avocado Egg Boats' are both American-style brunch dishes, making two of the three recipes too similar in style and occasion. The rubric explicitly states diversity is the key test and expects clearly different cuisines or styles (e.g., Mexican, salad, smoothie/toast — not overlapping styles).
column12: {"recipes":[{"name":"Avocado Toast","emoji":"🥑","desc":"Creamy mash on crisp toast.","add":[{"name_en":"Bread"},{"name_en":"Lemon"},{"name_en":"Salt"}]},{"name":"Avocado Pasta","emoji":"🍝","desc":"Blend into silky sauce.","add":[{"name_en":"Pasta"},{"name_en":"Garlic"},{"name_en":"Olive oil"}]},{"name":"Baked Avocado Eggs","emoji":"🍳","desc":"Warm halves with baked eggs.","add":[{"name_en":"Eggs"},{"name_en":"Pepper"},{"name_en":"Cheese"}]}]}
column16: While the recipes are practical, concise, and use avocado as a key ingredient, they fail the diversity test. Avocado Toast and Baked Avocado Eggs are both Western/brunch-style dishes, making two of the three recipes very similar in style. There is no representation of Mexican, Asian, Latin, or other distinct cuisines. The rubric explicitly requires spanning different cuisines or styles, which this output does not achieve.

column1: chicken thigh + Mexican items + vegetarian profile — conflict handling
column10: The output acknowledges the conflict and offers vegetarian alternatives (satisfying the conflict handling requirement). Added ingredients avoid tortillas, cilantro, and lime. Recipes are Mexican-themed. However, the first recipe's description ('Note: chicken conflicts—use veggie chicken instead.') is not genuinely descriptive of the dish — it only notes the conflict without describing what the recipe actually is, failing the 'concise and genuinely descriptive' requirement.
column12: {"recipes":[{"name":"Citrus Herb Veggie Wraps","emoji":"🌮","desc":"Note: you said vegetarian; use mushrooms instead","add":[{"name_en":"Mushrooms"},{"name_en":"Black beans"},{"name_en":"Avocado"}]},{"name":"Charred Street-Corn Tacos","emoji":"🌽","desc":"Quick skillet tacos with bright lime","add":[{"name_en":"Corn"},{"name_en":"Cotija cheese"},{"name_en":"Red onion"}]},{"name":"Cilantro-Lime Crispy Quesadillas","emoji":"🧀","desc":"Crispy pan-pressed, tangy, and cheesy","add":[{"name_en":"Cheddar cheese"},{"name_en":"Bell pepper"},{"name_en":"Sour cream"}]}]}
column16: The output partially handles the conflict by noting the vegetarian restriction and offering vegetarian alternatives (approach b). Added ingredients are appropriate (no tortillas, cilantro, or lime). Recipes are Mexican-leaning. However, the first recipe's description field is used for a conflict note rather than a genuine dish description, violating the 'genuinely descriptive' requirement. The conflict acknowledgment is also awkward and incomplete — only mentioned in one recipe's description field.

column1: bok choy + garlic/oyster sauce — authentic Chinese, not fusion
column12: {"recipes":[{"name":"Garlic Oyster Bok Choy Stir-Fry","emoji":"🥢","desc":"Note: you said vegetarian; use mushroom sauce","add":[{"name_en":"Sesame oil"},{"name_en":"Ginger"},{"name_en":"Soy sauce"}]},{"name":"Bok Choy Coconut Soup","emoji":"🍲","desc":"Creamy, soothing, and lightly savory broth","add":[{"name_en":"Coconut milk"},{"name_en":"Vegetable broth"},{"name_en":"Lime"}]},{"name":"Roasted Bok Choy with Crispy Tofu","emoji":"🔥","desc":"Charred greens with crunchy protein","add":[{"name_en":"Extra-firm tofu"},{"name_en":"Cornstarch"},{"name_en":"Chili flakes"}]}]}
column16: Only 1 of 3 recipes is recognizably Chinese (the stir-fry); the coconut soup is Southeast Asian/Thai fusion and the roasted tofu is Western-style. The rubric requires at least 2 of 3 to be recognizably Chinese. Additionally, the first recipe's description is a substitution note rather than a genuine dish description.
